data.title: Willow Lane amends transfer deal to let SPV buy 27.5% of founder shares/warrants for $1.75 each
data.description:
Willow Lane amended transfer agreement to let Goodrich ILMJS buy 27.5% of Sponsor's Founder Shares and warrants at $1.75 per share.

Key Highlights:

  • Amended and restated transfer agreement sets purchase of 1,272,885 Founder Shares and 1,101,986 private warrants.
  • Purchase price is $2,227,548.75 total, equal to $1.75 per Founder Share transferred.
  • Transfer must close on or before earlier of six months after Closing or 15 days after effective resale registration and lock-up expiry.
  • Transfer Securities will be held in escrow with Continental Stock Transfer & Trust until purchase is completed.
  • Supplemental disclosures update proxy statement ownership, quorum and vote descriptions tied to the Business Combination with Boost Run.

Original SEC Filing: Willow Lane Acquisition Corp. [ WLAC ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: PDF Solutions Ups Revolving Credit Facility to $75 Million, Adds Leverage-Based Fees With Wells Fargo
data.description:
PDF Solutions entered into a First Amendment to its Credit Agreement to increase its revolving credit facility to $75 million and introduce leverage-based commitment fees. The annual fee will be 0.50% when total debt to EBITDA is ≥2.50x, 0.35% when it is <2.50x but ≥0.50x, and 0.20% when it is <0.50x. All other material terms remain unchanged. The amendment is expected to enhance liquidity and better align pricing with the company’s leverage.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Amendment to revolving credit facility
  • Counterparty: Wells Fargo Bank, as administrative agent, and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 23 2026 / same
  • Reason: Increase liquidity and align fees to leverage

Original SEC Filing: PDF SOLUTIONS INC [ PDFS ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Michael D. Skaggs named Interim President and CEO of Tennessee Valley Authority
data.description:
Tennessee Valley Authority appointed Michael D. Skaggs as Interim President and Chief Executive Officer, effective April 24, 2026, succeeding Donald A. Moul who will retire July 1, 2026.

IndividualMichael D. SkaggsDonald A. Moul
RoleInterim President and Chief Executive OfficerPresident and Chief Executive Officer
Type of ChangeAppointedWill retire (succession)
Effective DateApril 24, 2026 (term ends April 24, 2027)July 1, 2026 (retirement)
ReasonNot disclosed (interim appointment following announced retirement)Retirement (previously announced)
Replacement InfoN/ASucceeded by Michael D. Skaggs (Interim)
Background DetailsJoined TVA 1994; held multiple senior roles including EVP & Advisor to CEO (Jun 2021–Jan 2022), EVP & COO (Oct 2018–Jun 2021), and various nuclear operations leadership positions; retired Jan 2022.Not disclosed
Board/Committee Role ChangesNot disclosedNot disclosed
CompensationAnnual salary $500,000; potential lump-sum Additional Compensation payable within two months after term if criteria met; removal only for Gross Misconduct except accelerated payments per Offer Letter.Not disclosed

Original SEC Filing: Tennessee Valley Authority [ TVC ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

data.title: Medicus Pharma Upsizes ATM Program to $50 Million With Maxim Group and Yorkville Securities
data.description:
Medicus Pharma amended its Equity Distribution Agreement to expand its at-the-market (ATM) equity offering capacity to up to $50 million. The amendment, executed with Maxim Group and Yorkville Securities, increases the prior aggregate amount of $15.35 million. Under the program, the company may sell common shares from time to time in at-the-market transactions; it has raised approximately $11.5 million to date. The move is intended to enhance financial flexibility for ongoing operations and strategic initiatives.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Amendment No. 1 to Equity Distribution Agreement (ATM offering upsize)
  • Counterparty: Maxim Group; Yorkville Securities
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 23 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Expand capacity to raise equity capital

Original SEC Filing: Medicus Pharma Ltd. [ MDCX ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Medicus Pharma Disclosed Failure to Satisfy a Continued Listing Rule or Standard
data.description:
On April 21, 2026, the company received notice from Nasdaq that its shares failed to maintain the $1.00 minimum bid price for 30 consecutive business days, violating Listing Rule 5550(a)(2). There is no immediate impact on trading, and the company has until October 19, 2026, to regain compliance by achieving a $1.00+ bid for at least 10 consecutive business days.

Original SEC Filing: Medicus Pharma Ltd. [ MDCX ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Orion Group Holdings Inc expected to post breakeven results a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Orion Group Holdings Inc , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 28 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Houston Texas-based company is expected to report a 5.1% increase in revenue to $198.201 million from $188.65 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 5 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Orion Group Holdings Inc is for breakeven results per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 5 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 9.0% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Orion Group Holdings Inc is $17.00, about 38.5% above its last closing price of $12.27

data.title: Highwoods Properties Inc expected to post earnings of 11 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Highwoods Properties Inc , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 28 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Raleigh North Carolina-based company is expected to report a 5.0% increase in revenue to $210.48 million from $200.38 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 5 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Highwoods Properties Inc is for earnings of 11 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy," 8 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 0.3% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Highwoods Properties Inc is $24.00, about 0.5% above its last closing price of $23.89

data.title: CEO.CA's Inside the Boardroom: TAG Oil Has a Pioneering Role in Egypt's National Energy Push
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - April 24, 2026) - CEO.CA ("CEO.CA"), the leading investor social network in junior resource and venture stocks, shares exclusive updates with CEOs of junior mining explorers.

Founded in 2012, CEO.CA, a wholly owned subsidiary of EarthLabs, Inc., is one of the most popular free financial websites and apps in Canada and for investors globally - with industry leading audience engagement and mobile functionality. Millions of people visit CEO.CA each year to connect with investors from around the world, share knowledge and view impactful stories about stocks, commodities, and emerging companies.

As a media partner at investor events around the world, CEO.CA provides coverage of the companies shaping the future of mining, meeting with industry leaders to learn more about their vision and strategy.

Meet the Executive Shaping the Mining Landscape

We caught up Abdel Badwi, Executive Chairman of TAG Oil Ltd. (OTCQB: TAOIF) (FSE: T0P) to discuss the political tailwind fueling their operations in Egypt's Western Desert.

See what investors are saying about TAG Oil https://ceo.ca/tao

TAG Oil Ltd.

(OTCQB: TAOIF) (FSE: T0P)

Tune into 'Inside the Boardroom' each week and be part of the conversation that's shaping the business landscape. Visit CEO.CA or our YouTube page for hundreds more executive interviews from CEO.CA here.

Interested in showcasing your company on 'Inside the Boardroom'? Get in touch with our team at sales@ceo.ca for further details and opportunities.

About CEO.CAThe leading community for investors & traders in junior resource & venture stocks. CEO.CA is one of the most popular free financial websites and apps in Canada and for small-cap investors globally -- with industry leading audience engagement and mobile functionality. Since 2012, CEO.CA has brought millions of investors together from over 164 countries to discuss their portfolio holdings and find new investment opportunities. Download our App on iOS or Android marketplace or visit us today at CEO.CA to set up your free account.

CEO.CA is a wholly owned subsidiary of EarthLabs, Inc.

For further information please contact:

CEO.CA

Email: hello@ceo.ca

Website: CEO.CA

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange ("TSXV"), OTC Best Market "(OTCQB") nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSXV) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Disclaimer

Cautionary Statement

The information regarding any issuer contained or referred to in any interviews conducted by CEO.CA has been furnished by such issuer directly, and neither CEO.CA nor any of its affiliates or principals assumes any responsibility for the accuracy or completeness of such information or for any failure by an issuer to ensure disclosure of events or facts which may affect the significance or accuracy of any such information.

data.title: NOTICE TO ATTEND THE ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING IN MAHA CAPITAL AB
data.description:
The shareholders in Maha Capital AB, reg. no. 559018-9543, are hereby given notice to attend the annual general meeting at 14.30 CEST on Tuesday 26 May 2026 at Setterwalls Advokatbyrå’s offices at Sturegatan 10 in Stockholm, Sweden. Registration for the meeting commences at 14.00 CEST.

Notice

Shareholders wishing to participate at the meeting must:

  • be entered in the shareholders’ register, kept by Euroclear Sweden AB (the Swedish Central Securities Depository & Clearing Organisation), on the record day which is Monday 18 May 2026; and
  • notify the company of their attendance and any assistants no later than Wednesday 20 May 2026. Notification can be made via letter to Setterwalls Advokatbyrå AB, Attn: Eric Torstensson, P.O. Box 1050, SE 101 39 Stockholm, Sweden or by e-mail to eric.torstensson@setterwalls.se.

Notification shall include full name, personal identification number or corporate registration number, address and daytime telephone number and, where appropriate, information about representative, proxy and assistants. The number of assistants may not be more than two (2). In order to facilitate entry to the meeting, notification should, where appropriate, be accompanied by powers of attorney, registration certificates and other documents of authority.

Personal data obtained from the share register kept by Euroclear Sweden AB, notices and attendance at the meeting and information on representatives, proxies and assistants will be used for registration, preparation of the voting list for the meeting and, where appropriate, the minutes of the meeting.

Nominee registered shares

Shareholders who have their shares registered in the name of a nominee must request temporary entry in the transcription of the share register kept by Euroclear Sweden AB in order to be entitled to participate and vote for their shares at the meeting. The shareholder must inform the nominee well in advance of Monday 18 May 2026 at which time the register entry must have been made. Voting rights registration that has been requested by the shareholder at such time that the registration has been completed by the nominee no later than Wednesday 20 May 2026, will, however, be taken into account in the preparation of the share register.

Proxy

A shareholder represented by proxy shall issue a power of attorney which shall be dated and signed by the shareholder. If issued by a legal entity, the power of attorney shall be accompanied by a registration certificate or, if not applicable, equivalent documents of authority. Power of attorney forms for those shareholders wishing to participate by proxy are available on the company’s website https://maha-capital.com/. The original version of the power of attorney shall also be presented at the meeting.

Proposed agenda

  • Opening of the meeting and election of chairman of the meeting;
  • Preparation and approval of the voting list;
  • Approval of the agenda;
  • Election of one (1) or two (2) persons who shall approve the minutes of the meeting;
  • Determination of whether the meeting has been duly convened;
  • Submission of the annual report and the auditor’s report and the consolidated financial statements and the auditor’s report on the group;
  • Resolution in respect of adoption of the profit and loss statement and the balance sheet and the consolidated profit and loss statement and the consolidated balance sheet;
  • Resolution in respect of allocation of the company’s profit or loss according to the adopted balance sheet;
  • Resolution in respect of the members of the board of directors’ and the managing director’s discharge from liability;
  • Determination of the number of members of the board of directors and the number of auditors and, where applicable, deputy auditors;
  • Determination of the fees payable to the members of the board of directors and the auditors;
  • Election of members of the board of directors, auditors and, where applicable, deputy auditors;
  • Approval of the remuneration report;
  • Resolution regarding an authorization for the board of directors to increase the share capital;
  • Resolution on amendments to the company’s articles of association;
  • Resolution on amendments to the policy for remuneration to the senior executives;
  • Resolution on amendments to the principles for the appointment of a nomination committee;
  • Closing of the meeting.

Proposed resolutions

Proposals in accordance with items 1, 10–12, and 17 will be presented by the nomination committee at the latest three (3) weeks prior to the annual general meeting.

Item 8. Allocation of the company’s profit or loss according to the adopted balance sheet

The board of directors proposes that the company’s available funds shall be carried forward in a new account and that no dividend shall be paid for the last financial year.

Item 13. Approval of the remuneration report

The board of directors proposes that the general meeting resolves to approve the board’s remuneration report in accordance with Chapter 8, Section 53a of the Swedish Companies Act.

Item 14. Resolution regarding authorization for the board of directors to increase the share capital

The board of directors proposes that the general meeting resolves on an authorization for the board of directors to – for the period up to the next annual general meeting and at one (1) or more occasions – resolve upon issuance of new shares, warrants and/or convertible debentures. Payment may be made in cash, in kind, through set-off of claims or otherwise be conditional. The company’s share capital may by support of the authorization be increased by an amount corresponding to 20 percent of the share capital and number of shares in the company as of the date the board of directors makes use of the authorization. Deviation from the shareholders’ preferential rights shall be allowed in situations where a directed issue is deemed more appropriate for the company due to timing, commercial or similar reasons, and in order to enable acquisitions. The chairman of the board of directors, the managing director, or anyone authorized by the board of directors, shall have the right to make any minor adjustments required in order to register this resolution.

Majority requirements

For a valid decision on the proposal on an authorization for the board of directors, as outlined above in this item 14, it is required that the proposal is supported by shareholders representing at least two-thirds (2/3) of the votes cast and the shares represented at the meeting.

Item 15. Resolution on amendments to the company’s articles of association

KEO Aggregator LP, which holds approximately 40 percent of the shares in the company, proposes that the meeting resolves on amendments to the company's articles of association as follows below. The Swedish Securities Council has assessed the proposal set forth below and has determined that the proposed amendments to the articles of association are not compatible with good practice in the Swedish securities market. Notwithstanding the assessment of the Swedish Securities Council, KEO Aggregator LP has decided to put forward the proposal, as KEO Aggregator LP consider that it creates a broader governance focused on credit control.

The proposal also entails minor linguistic adjustments to the articles of association and a renumbering of the sections of the articles of association as a result of the amendments proposed below. The complete articles of association, including all proposed amendments, are available on the company's website.

1. Amendment of § 6 Board of directors and majority requirements for certain decisions

§ 6 is amended by adjusting the limits on the number of board members and by introducing a special majority requirement for certain board decisions. The proposed wording appears below.

Proposed wording of § 6:

“The board of directors shall consist of six (6) members without deputy members.

The following resolutions shall only be validly adopted if supported by at least seventy-five (75) per cent of the directors present at the relevant board meeting:

  • any amendments to the company’s dividend policy, and any dividend proposal from the board;
  • any amendments to the company’s business plan; provided, however, that the re-adoption of an unchanged business plan shall require only a simple majority;
  • the appointment or dismissal of the Chief Executive Officer of the company;
  • any amendment to the Rules of Procedure of the board of directors; provided, however, that the annual re-adoption of an unchanged Rules of Procedure shall require only a simple majority;
  • without affecting the applicable decision-making procedures for related party transactions or the conflict of interest rules set out in the Swedish Companies Act (Sw: aktiebolagslagen (2005:551)), any transaction, arrangement or other dealing between the company or any of its subsidiaries on the one hand and a Related Party on the other hand (where “Related Party” shall be defined as (i) any shareholder holding, directly or indirectly, ten (10) per cent or more of the shares or votes in the company; (ii) any entity that is controlled by any such shareholder; or (iii) any member of the board of the company nominated (directly or indirectly) by such shareholder);
  • any material acquisition or divestiture, including any acquisition of shares, assets or a business (including mergers), with a consideration exceeding two (2) per cent of the company’s total consolidated assets, based on the most recent annual financial statement;
  • any incurrence of debt, exception made to (i) any intercompany loan or (ii) guarantees or debts necessary for the day to day operations that in aggregate do not exceed USD 15,000,000.00; and
  • any amendment, revision or replacement of the company's credit risk policy or equivalent credit risk management guidelines adopted by the board of directors, to the extent such guidelines govern credit decisions and credit exposures within the framework of the company's business.”

2. Introduction of new § 8 Majority requirements for certain general meeting decisions

A new § 8 is introduced, providing for special majority requirements for certain general meeting resolutions. The proposed wording appears below.

Proposed wording of § 8:

“Unless a different majority is required by mandatory law, the following resolutions shall only be validly adopted if supported by shareholders representing not less than ninety (90) per cent of both the votes cast and the shares represented at the relevant general meeting:

  • any amendment to § 3, § 6, or § 8 of these articles of association; and
  • any new issuance, with deviation from the shareholders' preferential rights, of shares, warrants, convertibles, or other share-related instruments. The aforementioned also applies to resolutions by the general shareholders’ meeting authorizing the board of directors to decide on such an issue and the subsequent approval by the general shareholders’ meeting of the board's decision on such an issue.”

3. Amendment of § 9 Place of shareholders’ meeting (numbering per proposed articles of association)

§ 9 is amended to remove the possibility of holding general meetings digitally. The proposed wording appears below.

Proposed wording of § 9: “The shareholders' meeting shall be held in Göteborg, Malmö, Stockholm, as determined by the board of directors.”

Majority requirements

For a valid decision on the proposal on amendments to the company’s articles of association, as outlined above in this item 15, it is required that the proposal is supported by shareholders representing at least two-thirds (2/3) of the votes cast and the shares represented at the meeting.

Item 16. Resolution on amendments to the policy for remuneration to the senior executives

The board of directors proposes that the meeting resolves on amendments to the policy for remuneration to the senior executives as follows.

The policy is adapted to the current company name and the company’s new business operations by replacement of industry-specific references to the oil and gas sector with broader references to the relevant market.

The new amended policy shall apply for the time being, but at the latest until the annual general meeting to be held in 2027. The new amended policy for remuneration to the senior executives is kept available in its entirety on the company’s website at https://maha-capital.com/.

Number of shares and votes in the company

The total number of shares in the company at the time of issuance of this notice is 351,991,889 and the total number of votes for all issued shares in the company is 351,991,889 votes. The company’s holding of own shares amounts, at the time of issuance of this notice, to 2,812,922 shares.

Shareholders’ right to request information

Pursuant to Chapter 7 section 32 of the Swedish Companies Act (Sw. aktiebolagslagen (2005:551)) the board of directors and the managing director are under a duty to, if any shareholder so requests and the board of directors deems that it can be made without material damage to the company, provide information, regarding circumstances which may affect the assessment of a matter on the agenda or of the company’s economic situation. Such duty to provide information also comprises the company’s relation to the other group companies, the consolidated financial statements and such circumstances regarding subsidiaries which are set out in the foregoing sentence.

Documentation

The financial accounts, auditor’s report, complete proposals for resolution and other documents to be dealt with at the general meeting will be kept available at the company’s office not later than three (3) weeks before the meeting. The documents will be sent free of charge to shareholders who so request and state their postal address. The documents will also be made available not later than the aforementioned date on the company’s website https://maha-capital.com/. All the above-mentioned documents will also be presented at the general meeting.

_____

Stockholm, April 2026

The board of directors

Contacts

Roberto Marchiori, CEO & CFO | Jakob Sintring, Head of IR

Phone: +46 8 611 05 11, E-mail: IR@maha-capital.com

About Maha Capital

Maha Capital AB (publ) is a listed technology-driven financial solutions provider focused on improving liquidity, security, transparency, and efficiency in B2B supply chain financing and corporate travel and expense management. Maha operates a unified digital ecosystem that enables buyers and suppliers to interact through complementary solutions designed to address the full spectrum of corporate payables. In addition, Maha holds 24 percent indirect equity stake in the Venezuelan oil company PetroUrdaneta. The shares are listed on Nasdaq Stockholm (MAHA-A). For more information, please visit the Company’s website at www.maha-capital.com.

Attachments

NOTICE TO ATTEND THE ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING IN MAHA CAPITAL AB

data.title: Intel's Big Ride - Heard on the Street Recap — WSJ
data.description:
By Heard Editors

No new talks yet. Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are being dispatched to Islamabad for talks with Iranian officials, but it was still unclear as of Friday afternoon if those talks would actually happen.

Tech jumps while Dow slips. Uncertainty on the state of the conflict weighed on the market, though was offset some by a broad rally across tech stocks. The Dow slipped 80 points to close at 49231, while the Nasdaq jumped 1.6%. Strong gains in chip stocks played a major role; Nvidia jumped more than 4% while AMD surged 14%.

Intel keeps the streak going. The chip maker's share price rocketed nearly 24% Friday following strong first-quarter earnings. The stock has now more than doubled so far this year as the company's prospects have improved, though its valuation against projected earnings remains at a record high.

Google ups its bet on Anthropic. Parent company Alphabet could invest as much as $40 billion under the new deal. Alphabet's stock rose 1.6% on Friday.

Powell probe ends. The Justice Department said it would end its criminal investigation of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. That should clear an obstacle that has stalled Kevin Warsh's confirmation as his successor.

This analysis comes from the Journal's Heard on the Street team. Subscribe to their free daily afternoon newsletter here.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Ventas Inc expected to post earnings of 12 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Ventas Inc , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 27 for the period ending March 31 2026

data.title: Northern Lights Completes Shares for Debt Settlement
data.description:
(TheNewswire)

Vancouver, BC – April 24, 2026 - TheNewswire – Northern Lights
Resources Corp. (“Northern Lights” orthe “Company”) announces that it has completed a shares for debtsettlement (the “DebtSettlement”) previously announced on April 9,2026.

Pursuant to the Debt Settlement, the Company has issuedan aggregate of 1,630,000 common shares (the “Settlement Shares”)at a deemed price of $0.12 per Settlement Share to settle $195,600 ofoutstanding indebtedness owing to certain creditors of the Company. As a result of the Debt Settlement, the indebtedness described abovehas been fully satisfied and extinguished.

All Settlement Shares issued pursuant to the DebtSettlement are subject to a statutory hold period of four months andone day from the date of issuance in accordance with applicablesecurities laws.

“The completion of the Debt Settlement strengthensthe Company’s balance sheet while preserving cash resources as wecontinue advancing our exploration projects.” stated Luka Capin,Chief Executive Officer of Northern Lights.

Of the total $195,600 of indebtedness settled, $174,000relates to arm’s length creditors, with the balance relating to aninsider of the Company.

Insider Participation

Certain insiders of the Company participated in theDebt Settlement representing $21,600 of the indebtedness settled and180,000 Settlement Shares issued.

The participation of such insiders constitutes arelated party transaction within the meaning of MultilateralInstrument 61-101 – Protection of Minority Security Holders inSpecial Transactions (“MI 61-101”). The Company relied onexemptions from the formal valuation and minority shareholder approvalrequirements pursuant to sections 5.5(a) and 5.7(1)(a) of MI 61-101,as neither the fair market value of the securities issued to insidersnor the consideration paid exceeds 25% of the Company’s marketcapitalization.

No new control person was created as a result of theDebt Settlement.

Qualified Person

Steven McMullan, P. Geo. supervised the preparation ofand reviewed and approved the scientific and technical informationcontained in this news release. Mr. McMullan is a qualified personas defined by National Instrument 43-101 - Standards of Disclosure forMineral Projects.

For Further Information

Luka Capin, Chief ExecutiveOfficer

Email: ir@northernlightsresources.com

Tel: +1 647 625 8669

About Northern LightsResources Corp.

Northern Lights Resources Corp is a growth-orientedexploration and development company advancing three key projects: theHoretzky Copper Project, located in the Babine Porphyry belt ofcentral British Columbia, the Pup Copper Project in the Yukon and the100% owned, Secret Pass Gold Project located in Arizona. NorthernLights also holds a 1% NSR royalty on the Medicine Springs SilverProject in Nevada 100% owned by Torex Gold Resources Inc.

Northern Lights Resources trades under the ticker of“NLR” on the CSE on the OTC under the ticker “NLRCF”and on theon the FSE  under the ticker symbol “0ZH0”. This and otherNorthern Lights Resources news releases can be viewed atwww.sedarplus.ca and www.northernlightsresources.com.

CAUTIONARY STATEMENTREGARDING FORWARD-LOOKING INFORMATION:

This news release contains forward-looking statementsand forward-looking information within the meaning of applicableCanadian securities laws (collectively, “forward-lookingstatements”). Forward-looking statements include, but are notlimited to, the Company’s future exploration activities andcorporate plans. Forward-looking statements are generally identifiedby words such as “anticipates”, “expects”, “intends”,“plans”, “believes”, or similar expressions, or statementsthat certain actions, events or results “may”, “could”,“would”, or “will” occur or be achieved.  Forward-lookingstatements are based on the opinions and estimates of management as ofthe date of this news release and are subject to known and unknownrisks, uncertainties and other factors that may cause actual resultsor events to differ materially from those anticipated in suchforward-looking statements. These risks and uncertainties include, butare not limited to, risks related to exploration activities, changesin market conditions, and other risks described in the Company’spublic disclosure filings available on SEDAR+.  Readers are cautionednot to place undue reliance on forward-looking statements. The Companydoes not undertake any obligation to update or revise anyforward-looking statements except as required by applicable securitieslaws.

data.title: Teledyne Technologies posts $1,560.1M revenue and $4.85 EPS in Q1
data.description:
Teledyne Technologies reported first-quarter results showing revenue of $1,560.1 million and diluted earnings per share of $4.85, reflecting year‑over‑year growth in both top line and profitability. The company cited stronger demand across Digital Imaging, Instrumentation and Aerospace & Defense that supported a 7.6% rise in net sales and a 20.3% increase in net income attributable to Teledyne. Management noted continued investment in R&D and planned capital expenditures to support product development and manufacturing capacity.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $1,560.1 million, up 7.6% versus prior year (Q1 2025: $1,449.9M)
  • Gross profit: $673.8 million (as reported)
  • Operating income: $294.2 million, up 13.5% versus prior year (Q1 2025: $259.3M)
  • Net income attributable to Teledyne: $226.8 million, up 20.3% versus prior year (Q1 2025: $188.6M)
  • Diluted earnings per common share: $4.85, versus $3.99 prior year

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Strength across Digital Imaging, Instrumentation and Aerospace & Defense segments supported overall sales growth.
  • Market demand: Increased defense and offshore energy demand boosted sales in Marine, Environmental and Defense Electronics markets.
  • Product momentum: Digital Imaging led growth with higher sales of infrared detectors, surveillance equipment and unmanned systems.
  • Operational efficiency: Improved margins were attributed to favorable product mix, cost containment and integration of recent acquisitions.
  • Capital and R&D plans: Approximately $150 million of planned capital expenditures for 2026 and increased R&D spending to support product development and manufacturing upgrades.

Original SEC Filing: TELEDYNE TECHNOLOGIES INC [ TDY ] - 10-Q - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Helix Energy Solutions Group Q1: Revenue $287.9M; EPS $(0.09)
data.description:
Helix Energy Solutions Group reported first-quarter revenues of $287.946 million and a net loss of $13.406 million, or $(0.09) per diluted share, driven by higher segment revenues but lower margins. Gross profit contracted to $8.828 million (3% margin), turning operating results negative and producing a swing to an operating loss of $(13.315) million versus operating income in the prior-year quarter. Management cites higher activity in robotics, well intervention and shallow water abandonment alongside vessel reactivations as drivers of revenue growth despite margin pressure.

Financial Highlights

  • Net revenues: $287.946 million, up 4% versus Q1 2025.
  • Gross profit: $8.828 million (3% margin), down $18.710 million versus Q1 2025 (10% margin in Q1 2025).
  • Income (loss) from operations: $(13.315) million, a swing from $8.172 million in Q1 2025.
  • Net income (loss): $(13.406) million, compared with net income of $3.072 million in Q1 2025.
  • Diluted earnings per share: $(0.09); basic EPS also $(0.09). Prior-year comparable EPS was $0.02.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth by segment: Consolidated revenues were up 4% year-over-year, driven by Well Intervention (+6%), Robotics (+22%) and Shallow Water Abandonment (+26%).
  • Demand shift toward robotics: Strong robotics demand with higher ROV and trencher utilization and increased chartered vessel days, reflecting momentum in trenching and site preparation for offshore wind projects.
  • Fleet utilization: Improved utilization across key fleets — Well Intervention vessels at 82% (versus 67% prior), Robotics assets at 56% (versus 51%), and Shallow Water Abandonment vessels at 35% (versus 30%).
  • Backlog and customer concentration: Backlog stands at approximately $1.2 billion, with about $551 million expected to be recognized for the remainder of 2026 and revenues concentrated among major customers including Shell, Subsea 7, Petrobras, Talos, NKT and CNR.
  • Operational milestones and outlook: Reactivation and mobilization of key vessels (Q7000, Seawell), completion of Thunder Hawk workover, and an outlook supported by higher commodity prices and increased enforcement of UK abandonment activity.

Original SEC Filing: HELIX ENERGY SOLUTIONS GROUP INC [ HLX ] - 10-Q - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Healthcare Services Group 10-Q: Revenue $462.8M, EPS $0.37
data.description:
Healthcare Services Group reported quarterly revenue of $462.8 million and diluted earnings per share of $0.37, reflecting modest revenue growth and improved profitability. The company cited client wins, strong retention, contractual price increases and pass-through costs as drivers of the revenue increase and margin improvement. Net income rose materially year-over-year, supported by lower SG&A and updated self-insurance estimates.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $462.8 million, up 3.4% versus prior year.
  • Gross Profit: $75.8 million (as provided).
  • Operating Income: $33.9 million (as provided).
  • Net Income: $26.1 million, up 51.3% versus prior year.
  • Diluted net income per common share: $0.37; basic $0.37; weighted average diluted shares 71,049.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Growth was driven by Environmental Services (EVS) and Dietary operations, with EVS growing ~6.1% and Dietary ~1.3% year-over-year.
  • Channel and mix: EVS served roughly 2,300 sites (~45% of revenue) while Dietary served about 1,600 sites (~55% of revenue), indicating steady portfolio mix.
  • Operational efficiency: Segment expense ratios improved as labor costs declined across EVS and Dietary, each reducing segment expense ratios by about 1.4%.
  • Quality and retention: Company cited client wins and strong retention tied to consistent service execution and contractual price/pass-through increases.
  • Risk management: Updated self-insurance loss estimates lowered costs by $4.7 million, benefiting service margins in the quarter.

Original SEC Filing: HEALTHCARE SERVICES GROUP INC [ HCSG ] - 10-Q - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Five Point Holdings 10-Q: Revenues $13.6M, EPS (Basic) $(0.03)
data.description:
Five Point Holdings reported quarterly revenues of $13.6 million and a net loss attributable to the company of $5.0 million, with basic and diluted net loss per Class A share of $(0.03), reflecting limited land sale closings in the quarter and higher operating costs. Revenue growth was modest year-over-year, supported by management services including Hearthstone, while results were pressured by timing of land sales and equity losses. Management emphasized cash generation, SG&A control and aligning capital spending with near-term revenue expectations.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $13.6 million, up $0.4 million (3.2%) year-over-year, driven by management services (Hearthstone) offset by lower Great Park incentive fees.
  • Gross profit: $(9.6) million (implied; total revenues $13.6M less total costs and expenses $23.2M), reflecting higher costs versus revenue.
  • Operating income (loss): $(5.9) million (loss before income tax benefit), impacted by selling, general and administrative expenses of $14.7 million and higher management costs.
  • Net (loss) income: $(5.0) million, versus $60.6 million in the prior-year quarter; decline driven by timing of land sales and equity losses.
  • Net (loss) income attributable to the company per Class A share: Basic $(0.03), Diluted $(0.03).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix shift: Management services and Hearthstone fees were primary revenue drivers this quarter as residential land sales were minimal.
  • Operational cadence: Land sale activity is expected to be weighted to the third and fourth quarters, with few significant closings in Q1 that negatively impacted current results.
  • Hearthstone momentum: Hearthstone expanded with two new funds totaling approximately $600 million in commitments and reported about $3.4 billion in assets under management, supporting capital-light growth.
  • Community absorption: Homebuilder sales showed mixed trends—Valencia recorded 90 homes versus 69 year-over-year, while Great Park had 82 versus 233 year-over-year—indicating moderated demand in some markets.
  • Execution priorities: Management is focused on generating cash flow, controlling SG&A, aligning capital spending with near-term revenue, and pursuing strategic partnerships to support recovery in land sale activity.

Original SEC Filing: Five Point Holdings, LLC [ FPH ] - 10-Q - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Haggai Alon Discloses Investment at SMX (Security Matters) with 3.08% Stake
data.description:
Haggai Alon, Chairman and CEO of SMX (Security Matters) Public Limited Company, disclosed beneficial ownership of 3.08% of the company’s ordinary shares. The filing, dated Apr. 24, 2026, reflects 224,501 shares that settled from previously granted restricted stock units. The position stems from compensation awards rather than open-market purchases.

Investor Intent

Alon received the shares as compensation under the company’s 2022 Incentive Equity Plan and may receive more awards under that plan. He states he acquired the securities “for compensatory purposes pursuant to the Incentive Plan” and “does not currently have any plans or proposals” for actions listed in Item 4(a)-(j). The filing notes he could change his plans in the future, but it sets no concrete targets or governance moves.

Investor's Background

Haggai Alon serves as Chairman of the Board and Chief Executive Officer of SMX (Security Matters) Public Limited Company. He is an Israeli executive known for leading companies focused on track-and-trace and material authentication technologies. Alon’s approach centers on building strategic positions in his own ventures and aligning equity incentives with operational milestones.

Original SEC Filing: SMX (Security Matters) Public Ltd Co [ SMX ] - SCHEDULE 13D - Apr. 24, 2026

data.title: Ford Is in Talks With China's Geely. Why the 'Most American' Auto Maker Is Looking Abroad. — Barrons.com
data.description:
Al Root

Ford Motor, the most American car maker, is thinking about going Chinese.

The Wall Street Journal reported Friday that Ford Motor held talks with Chinese auto maker Geely that could result in Geely technology in the U.S.

Ford didn't immediately respond to a request for comment. "We always keep an open mind when it comes to exploring cooperative opportunities," the company told the WSJ.

This isn't a great idea.

For starters, Chinese cars are effectively banned from the U.S. by high tariffs. Investors and economists can argue about the effectiveness and fairness of that policy, but it has insulated America from cars built with low-cost Chinese labor from flooding the market.

The insulated market has been good for auto industry profits. General Motors is expected to produce an operating profit margin of about 8% in 2026. Renault, Volkswagen, and China's BYD are expected to earn closer to 5%.

In Europe, BYD now has about 7% market share. A year ago, its share was less than 3%. BYD's exports grew 65% in March, accounting for 40% of sales. Part of the reason for export growth is slowing domestic sales.

Having those cars in the U.S. without tariffs would likely push down consumer prices, but it would upset the domestic supply-demand balance.

China, of course, can invest directly in the U.S., building plants and dealership networks, the way Toyota Motor did, but that takes billions of dollars and years. It can pay off. Toyota started selling cars in the U.S. in the 1950s. It beat General Motors for the U.S. sales crown in 2021.

Any agreement with Geely could also cause complications for Ford. CEO Jim Farley often refers to Ford as the "most American" car company. Ford makes roughly 80% of the cars it sells in the U.S. domestically, far above the industry average of about 50%. It also employs the most UAW Union members, according to the company.

To be sure, Chinese imports aren't unprecedented. GM builds the Buick Envision in China. It is a Buick, though, not a Chinese brand. And GM is shifting production to Kansas City to avoid tariffs.

Ford stock dropped 0.8% on Friday, closing at $12.38, while the S&P 500 gained 0.8%. The loss left shares up 23% over the past 12 months. Investors will hear from Ford this coming week. It reports first-quarter earnings on Apr. 29.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Westamerica Increases Stock Repurchase Authorization by 2.0M Shares, Leaves 2.785M Shares Available
data.description:
Westamerica increased its stock repurchase authorization by 2,000,000 shares, leaving 2,785,023 shares available under the expanded plan (11.8% of shares outstanding).

Key Highlights:

  • Board approved a 2,000,000-share increase to the repurchase authorization to bolster capital management.
  • After prior purchases, 2,785,023 shares remain available under the plan, equal to 11.8% of common stock.
  • Repurchases may occur on the open market or in private transactions through Dec 31, 2026.
  • Board cites financial strength, conservative risk profile and reliable earnings as rationale for the increase.

data.title: Surf Air Mobility Arranges Up to $15 Million Secured Note With LamVen, 12.5% Rate to 2029
data.description:
Surf Air Mobility entered into a secured promissory note with LamVen for financing of up to $15 million to support liquidity and operations. Advances are limited to $5 million per consecutive 90-day period and the note matures on Apr 20, 2029. The debt carries a 12.5% annual interest rate, payable monthly in cash or, at the company’s election, in common stock or pre-funded warrants valued at $1.274 per share, subject to beneficial ownership limits. A $1.5 million origination fee is due on the later of Jul 19, 2026 or the first advance, and the note is secured by aircraft assets; remedies are limited to the borrowers.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Secured promissory note financing facility up to $15 million
  • Counterparty: LamVen
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 20 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Through Apr 20 2029
  • Reason: Bolster liquidity and support fleet operations

Original SEC Filing: SURF AIR MOBILITY INC. [ SRFM ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

data.title: Palomino Laboratories Raises $15.1 Million in Private Placement, Up to $30 Million Target
data.description:
Palomino Laboratories completed an initial closing of a private placement on Apr 20, 2026, selling 3,773,853 common shares at $4.00 per share for $15,095,412, with additional closings allowed through Apr 30, 2026 for up to $30,000,000. The company entered Subscription Agreements with accredited investors and granted registration rights to purchasers, committing to file a Form S-1 and pursue effectiveness within 120 days, with follow-on and piggyback rights. Palomino also put in place a Laidlaw-specific registration rights agreement that will activate when it becomes eligible to use Form S-3, and engaged Laidlaw & Company (UK) as placement agent with cash fees, expense reimbursements, and five-year warrants. The financing is intended to bolster liquidity and support growth initiatives.

Agreement 1: Palomino Laboratories Raises $15.1 Million in Private Placement, Up to $30 Million Target

  • Agreement type: Subscription Agreements for private placement of common stock
  • Counterparty: Accredited investors
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 20 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Through Apr 30 2026
  • Reason: Raise growth capital and strengthen liquidity

Agreement 2: Palomino Laboratories Grants Investors Registration Rights for Private Placement Shares

  • Agreement type: Registration Rights Agreement for resale of common stock
  • Counterparty: Purchasers in the Offering
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 20 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Provide resale liquidity to investors

Agreement 3: Palomino Laboratories Sets Laidlaw Registration Rights Upon Future S-3 Eligibility

data.title: NexGel Disclosed Failure to Satisfy a Continued Listing Rule or Standard
data.description:
On April 22, 2026, the company received a Nasdaq deficiency letter for not maintaining the $1.00 minimum bid price for 30 consecutive business days under Listing Rule 5550(a)(2). NexGel has 180 days, until October 19, 2026, to regain compliance, with trading continuing on the Nasdaq Capital Market under NXGL and a potential second 180-day period or reverse split if needed.

Original SEC Filing: NEXGEL, INC. [ NXGL ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Ivy Brown resigns from Chefs' Warehouse board
data.description:
Chefs' Warehouse announced that Ivy Brown resigned from its Board of Directors, effective April 20, 2026.

IndividualIvy Brown
RoleMember, Board of Directors
Type of ChangeResigned
Effective DateApril 20, 2026
ReasonPersonal reasons; not due to disagreement with the Company
Replacement InfoCompany does not plan to introduce a substitute nominee for the May 8, 2026 annual meeting; seat will remain vacant until filled or Board size reduced
Background DetailsNot disclosed
Board/Committee Role ChangesNot disclosed

Original SEC Filing: Chefs' Warehouse, Inc. [ CHEF ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026

data.title: IonQ receives FTC second request, extending HSR waiting period for SkyWater merger
data.description:
IonQ received an FTC second request that extends the HSR waiting period for its proposed SkyWater merger.

Key Highlights:

  • FTC issued a Second Request to IonQ and SkyWater on April 24, 2026, extending the HSR waiting period.
  • The waiting period now runs 30 days after both parties substantially comply, unless extended or terminated earlier.
  • IonQ and SkyWater expect to respond promptly and remain cooperative with the FTC review.
  • The Mergers are still expected to close in Q2 or Q3 2026, subject to HSR expiration and other customary closing conditions.

Original SEC Filing: IonQ, Inc. [ IONQ ] - 8-K - Apr. 24, 2026
